title: DevOps Engineering Upskiller
source: Authored for the Salareen corporate catalog; structured to mirror the UK apprenticeship standard format.

knowledge.K1: What DevOps is: a culture and set of practices uniting development and operations.
knowledge.K2: Version control and collaboration workflows: Git, branching and pull requests.
knowledge.K3: Continuous integration: automated build and test on every change.
knowledge.K5: Infrastructure as code for reproducible environments.
knowledge.K6: Containers and Docker for portable, isolated applications.
knowledge.K7: Orchestration with Kubernetes for scale and resilience.
knowledge.K9: Reliability and incident response, including blameless post-mortems.
knowledge.K10: Security in the pipeline (DevSecOps): scanning, secrets and least privilege.
knowledge.K11: Automation of toil and continuous improvement.
knowledge.K12: Measuring delivery speed and production stability.

skills.S1: Collaborate using Git branching, pull requests and review.
skills.S2: Configure continuous integration with automated tests.
skills.S3: Build automated delivery and deployment pipelines.
skills.S5: Build and run applications in containers.
skills.S6: Deploy and operate workloads on Kubernetes.
skills.S7: Implement metrics, logs, traces and alerting.
skills.S9: Embed security scanning and secrets management in the pipeline.
skills.S11: Measure and improve delivery and stability metrics.
skills.S12: Design for redundancy, recovery and cost-awareness.

behaviours.B2: Takes ownership of reliability and outcomes.
behaviours.B3: Collaborates across development and operations with shared ownership.
behaviours.B4: Acts with integrity on security and compliance.
behaviours.B5: Stays calm and effective during incidents and complexity.

Duty 2.statement: Deliver and deploy software through automated pipelines.

Duty 4.statement: Operate workloads at scale with Kubernetes.

Duty 5.statement: Observe, respond to incidents and improve reliability.
